subject: Geology
title: Dissolution
description: A quick, practical guide to Dissolution.

hook.text: Rainwater is slightly acidic. Over thousands of years, it dissolves limestone, carving caves and sinkholes.

buildup.text: Dissolution is chemical weathering where minerals dissolve in water. Carbonic acid in rain attacks carbonate rocks.

discovery.text: Limestone dissolves because calcium carbonate reacts with carbonic acid, forming soluble calcium bicarbonate.

twist.text: Karst landscapes with caves, sinkholes, and disappearing rivers are all products of dissolution over long periods.

climax.text: Dissolution reshapes entire regions. The caves of Kentucky and the cenotes of Mexico were carved by acidic water.

punchline.text: Weak acid and patience carve caverns.

quiz.question: What type of rock is most affected by dissolution?
